title: Fix terminal flicker when exiting fullscreen
prompt:
Bug: The terminal opened from the top-right of the app flickers/oscillates when collapsing out of fullscreen.

Repro:
1. Open the terminal (top-right).
2. Click the fullscreen button — works fine, terminal goes fullscreen.
3. Click the shrink/collapse (exit-fullscreen) button — BUG: the terminal rapidly toggles back and forth between fullscreen and collapsed (flicker/jump loop) instead of settling into the collapsed state.

Goal: Clicking the collapse button must cleanly return the terminal to its non-fullscreen size with no oscillation.

Investigation hints:
- The fullscreen/expand toggle state and handlers are likely in web-ui/src/components/card-detail-view.tsx (isBottomTerminalExpanded / onBottomTerminalToggleExpand, isDiffExpanded / handleToggleDiffExpand), web-ui/src/components/detail-panels/agent-terminal-panel.tsx, and web-ui/src/hooks/use-home-sidebar-agent-panel.tsx.
- This kind of oscillation is usually a feedback loop: a useEffect / ResizeObserver / layout measurement that re-sets the expand state after the user toggles it, or two sources of truth for the fullscreen flag fighting each other, or a layout reflow that retriggers the toggle handler.

Requirements:
1. Identify the root cause of the toggle oscillation (use systematic debugging — find the actual feedback loop, do not just patch symptoms).
2. Fix it so exiting fullscreen is stable. Open and fullscreen behavior must keep working.
3. Add or update a test that covers exiting fullscreen without re-triggering the expanded state, if feasible.
4. Ensure type-check, lint, and tests pass.
